narrative.executive_summary: Frontier labs spent the week racing to compress the gap between model and product, with Anthropic and OpenAI both shipping agent-runtime primitives rather than raw model upgrades. Anthropic's managed-agent SDK and OpenAI's persistent-task API anchor a clear pattern: capability discovery is moving from model benchmarks to harness ergonomics. Google held the line on Gemini's enterprise narrative with a Vertex pricing reset, while xAI used a quiet model week to expand Colossus capacity and tie Grok to X's commerce surface. Open source mirrored the labs — the week's biggest star deltas concentrated in agent frameworks and MCP tooling, not new base models. The composite read is that 2026's frontier is being defined by who owns the loop between the model and the deployed task, not by the underlying weights themselves.
narrative.dominant_theme: Agent runtimes and harnesses replaced raw model releases as the frontier surface this week.
narrative.market_mood_rationale: No frontier model launches landed; the four labs converged on agent-platform investment, which signals that capability arbitrage has moved one layer up the stack.

biggest_move.move: Released Claude Agent SDK 1.0 on 2026-05-07 with managed-agent runtime, persistent memory, and a first-party harness for hosting long-running tool-using agents.
biggest_move.signal: Anthropic is staking out agent infrastructure as a product moat parallel to the model itself — competing with OpenAI's Assistants API on platform completeness, not raw capability.

narrative.competitive_frontier: Frontier shifted from model-quality benchmarks to runtime ergonomics — observability, memory, sandboxing, and cost levers. Anthropic and OpenAI now ship overlapping platform stacks; differentiation is moving to developer experience.
narrative.open_source_signal: MCP server repos and agent-framework repos dominated weekly star growth, while base-model and inference repos saw flat or negative deltas. Practitioner energy is concentrated on glue code between models and tasks, not on model alternatives.

strategic_takeaways.headline: Agent runtimes are the new model release.
strategic_takeaways.explanation: Both Anthropic and OpenAI shipped runtime primitives this week rather than model upgrades. Allocators should treat agent-platform completeness as the relevant capability axis for the next two quarters.

strategic_takeaways.headline: Open source is consolidating around MCP.
strategic_takeaways.explanation: Five of the week's top ten trending repos are MCP-related. The protocol layer is winning developer attention faster than any single agent framework, which compresses the moat for proprietary connector marketplaces.

strategic_takeaways.headline: Compute capacity is the silent story.
strategic_takeaways.explanation: xAI's Colossus expansion and Anthropic's Atlas region announcement both reinforce that frontier scale-out is now a quarterly cadence — and capex constraints are an underweighted risk to model roadmaps.

to_watch.item: Google I/O keynote — expected Gemini Enterprise pricing, robotics demo, and possible Gemma 3 weights drop.
OpenAI Spring DevDay rumored for late May — likely realtime API GA and Codex agent updates.
Whether the MCP server ecosystem starts to fragment into commercial vs open-source camps as registries proliferate.

content_angles.newsletter_angle: Frame the week as the moment the industry stopped competing on model quality and started competing on the harness around the model — and explain why that's a structurally different game.
content_angles.linkedin_hook: Anthropic just released an Agent SDK that essentially boxes up the daily workflow of an analyst — but most takes on it are dangerous.

anthropic.fact: Released Claude Agent SDK 1.0 with managed-agent runtime, persistent memory, and first-party harness.
anthropic.metric: Supports 200K+ context with persistent memory across sessions

anthropic.fact: Announced Atlas — a new sovereign-cloud region pairing with AWS for EU enterprise deployments.

openai.fact: Shipped persistent-task API beta — agents can now suspend and resume long-running tool sequences with state preserved server-side.
Cut o4-mini API pricing 35% to $0.65 in / $2.60 out per 1M tokens.

google.fact: Repriced Gemini 2.5 Pro on Vertex with a new enterprise tier — 30% discount for >$50K monthly committed spend.
Quietly released Gemma 3 8B-it preview weights to Hugging Face ahead of I/O.

xai.fact: Announced Colossus 2 capacity expansion to 250K H200 equivalents, online by Q3 2026.
Tied Grok to X commerce — agents can now execute purchases on X via natural language.

items.fact: Anthropic's Agent SDK ships first-party verticalized templates for finance, legal, and sales workflows.
OpenAI Assistants API gained native multi-agent orchestration with shared memory pools.
LangChain shipped LangSmith Agent Audit — runtime governance and identity-aware audit trails.

agent_capabilities.pattern: Every major agent framework added governance primitives this week — identity, audit, and observability are now table-stakes, not differentiators.

items.fact: Claude Code 2.1 added native MCP server discovery and a built-in agent scheduling skill.
Cursor 0.50 introduced sandboxed tool execution and per-project skill registries.
Gemini CLI gained Vertex-managed agents in preview.

repos.description: Official MCP server reference implementations — biggest weekly delta of the period.
Anthropic Agent SDK launch repo — almost entirely net-new stars.
Lightweight multi-agent orchestration from OpenAI, refreshed with Assistants integration.
Browser-automation agent with Playwright + LLM grounding.
Minimal agent library with code-first tool calling.
Local inference workhorse — flat delta confirms attention moved up the stack.
